title: Garmin unveils CIRQA Smart Band, its first screen-free fitness tracker
description: Garmin has announced its first screen-free fitness tracker called the CIRQA Smart Band. Unlike similar alternatives in the market, Garmin’s offering does not require a subscription to access advanced fitness and wellness features. The Garmin CIRQA Smart Band is claimed to deliver up to 10 days of battery life from a single charge even with 24/7 health monitoring. It features a fiber-reinforced polymer case with a fabric strap and can be worn around the wrist or the arm. The module weighs 14.8 grams and gets a 5ATM rating. As for health and fitness tracking, the smart band...

title: One UI 9 beta lets Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra users hide picture-in-picture videos with Privacy Display
description: Samsung introduced the Privacy Display feature with the Galaxy S26 Ultra, which makes it hard for others to see the contents on the phone's screen when viewed from an angle. With the fourth One UI 9 beta released last week, Samsung upgraded Privacy Display. The One UI 9 beta update for the Galaxy S26 Ultra brings picture-in-picture (PiP) video support for the Privacy Display, meaning that when you start watching a video in PiP mode with Privacy Display enabled, only that PiP window is obscured, while the contents on the rest of the screen remain visible at off-angle. You can watch the...

title: OnePlus N6x design and color options teased
description: Last week, OnePlus teased the arrival of a new N-series smartphone in India, the OnePlus N6x. Now, the company has shared another teaser, offering a sneak peek at the handset's design and color options. While OnePlus is yet to announce the launch date for the N6x in India, the latest teaser confirms that the smartphone will be available in light blue and maroon color options. The teaser also offers a glimpse of the handset's design, revealing a flat rear panel and a flat frame. It features a pill-shaped rear camera module with an LED flash positioned alongside it, marking a...

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch9 and Watch Ultra 2 full specs and pricing leak
description: Samsung is set to host its Galaxy Unpacked event in London later today, where it is expected to unveil its next-generation foldables alongside the Galaxy Watch9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. Ahead of the launch, the complete specs and pricing of the upcoming smartwatches have leaked. As per the leak, the Galaxy Watch9 and the Watch Ultra 2 will be powered by the Snapdragon Wear Elite chipset, paired with 2GB of RAM. The Galaxy Watch9 is said to offer 32GB of onboard storage, while the Watch Ultra 2 could come with 64GB of storage. The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 is tipped to feature a...

title: Xiaomi Mix Fold 5, global Redmi Note 17 and Redmi Note 17 Pro Max get certified
description: Xiaomi is working on its next foldable smartphone, which we're tentatively calling the Xiaomi Mix Fold 5. It has now allegedly been certified for sale in China by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT) with the model number 2608BPX34C. The device is expected to have a wider form factor than its predecessors, similar to the Galaxy Z Fold8 and iPhone Ultra. Its folding screen is apparently going to be 7.6" diagonally. It is rumored to be powered by Xiaomi's own Xring O3 SoC, the successor to the original Xring O1 from last year. The Mix Fold 5 should launch running...

title: This is what the Asus ROG Phone 10 and ROG Phone 10 Pro could have been
description: Asus launched the ROG Phone 9 and the ROG Phone 9 Pro in 2024, and has since decided to stop releasing new phones. So there has been no ROG Phone 10, and there probably never will be. However, thanks to some leaked firmware, we now know what we could have expected had the ROG Phone 10 and ROG Phone 10 Pro actually launched. Unsurprisingly, both would have been powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, paired with 5G connectivity, UFS 4.0 storage, and LPDDR5X RAM. Wi-Fi 7 was supported too. The ROG Phone 10 Pro would have had a 50MP main camera using OmniVision’s OV50Q...

title: Samsung unveils AI-powered Health Assistant
description: Today Samsung has announced the Health Assistant, which it bills as the "first fully integrated AI-powered personal health assistant". It is now available in beta for eligible US users of Samsung Health. Samsung promises that the Health Assistant will help you achieve your health and wellness goals by combining important health data with insights that empower you to ask questions about your health and "gain access to rich, personalized health guidance and recommendations". Health Assistant connects your health information from Samsung Health's "five pillars of wellness" - sleep,...

title: Google launches Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
description: Today Google is launching its newest Gemini models. Gemini 3.6 Flash is described by the company as its "workhorse model that delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance". It's supposed to be more efficient and better quality than its predecessor. Google boasts that customers "have found it particularly capable at multimodal tasks like document parsing, chart and data analysis, and report drafting". Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite is "designed for both low-latency tasks and tasks where high throughput is critical for developer workflows, like agentic search and document...

title: Apple Upgrade program is coming next week with up to 36-month payment plans
description: According to a new report from Bloomberg, Apple is planning to launch a new device leasing program in partnership with Klarna. It will be called Apple Upgrade and will require a soft credit check. It will apparently work like a subscription, with iPhone and Apple Watch leases running for 24 months and iPad and Mac leases running for 36 months. The Apple Watch SE, the base iPad, the iPhone 16, and the MacBook Neo will be excluded from the program. You will be able to pay off the device early and upgrade early to a new model, as well as decide whether to return or keep the device at the end...

title: Xiaomi Redmi Note 15 Pro 4G review
Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is on hold
description: The bidding war for Warner Bros. Discovery was won by Paramount Skydance, which forced Netflix to drop out. The deal has however been put on hold by a judge. Twelve US state attorneys general requested a temporary restraining order to block Paramount Skydance from closing the deal, and US District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin of the Northern District of California granted the request. The deal is now effectively on hold for the next 14 days. On August 3, the judge will hold a hearing on the state attorneys general's motion for a preliminary injunction, which would freeze the deal until...

title: Apple's 20th anniversary iPhone may offer a 7-inch screen
description: Apple's iPhone will celebrate its 20th anniversary next year, and the company is rumored to mark the occasion with a major redesign for its iPhone Pro lineup. The upcoming models are likely to go by the iPhone 20 moniker and are said to feature an edge-to-edge quad-curved display and solid-state buttons. Well-known tipster Digital Chat Station has now claimed that Apple is testing a 6.96-inch display, which could make its way to the iPhone 20 Pro Max. Image generated using Gemini AI While that's only 0.06 inches larger than the panel on the current iPhone 17 Pro Max, the tipster...

title: Report: TSMC is planning to raise chip prices in 2027
description: The rising prices of semiconductor products are not showing signs of slowing down. Nikkei Asia reports that TSMC will raise prices by up to 10% starting next year, which is bad news for its clients – the likes of Nvidia, Apple, Qualcomm, AMD and Intel – and that in turn will be bad news for their customers. The prices for TSMC’s advanced nodes will go up by 5% to 10%, depending on the type of chip and the company buying it. This will make chipsets for future smartphones more expensive. Tablets, laptops, smartwatches and so on will likely be affected as well. The bad news is that we’re...

title: Samsung introduces the Galaxy Card to take on Apple Card
description: Samsung has introduced the Galaxy Card in the US, a new credit card that lets customers earn cash rewards on eligible purchases. The company has partnered with Barclays US Consumer Bank and Visa to offer the Galaxy Card. It will be available as both a physical metal card featuring Samsung's black logo and a virtual card. Applications open on July 22, with eligible customers able to apply online or at Samsung retail stores across the US. New cardholders can earn a $200 welcome bonus by spending $2,000 within the first 90 days of opening their account. The Galaxy Card offers 5%...

title: vivo S2 teaser image leaks alongside its specs
description: Back in May we heard that vivo was working on restarting the S series in India with the S2. This will be the follow-up to the S1, which was launched in 2019, but after so much time it's obviously not going to be a proper successor. Today a teaser image for the upcoming device has been leaked and you can see it below. The phone is rumored to be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 chipset. It's also said to come with a 6.59-inch OLED display with "1.5K" resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate, an in-display fingerprint scanner, a 50MP main camera, a 50MP periscope telephoto camera, an 8MP...

title: Indian smartphone market shipments fell 13% in Q2 according to Omdia
description: A few days ago Counterpoint Research released its report on the Indian smartphone market for the second quarter of this year (April to June), and now Omdia has done the same. According to Omdia's data, the overall smartphone shipments in India fell by 13% in Q2 compared to the same period last year, to 33.9 million units. Of course, rising memory prices are primarily to blame, as they have resulted in "reduced affordability for mass-market consumers". Omdia says vivo shipped 6.3 million smartphones for a leading 18% market share, down 23% year-on-year from 8.1 million and 21%,...

title: Honor 600 Pro gets a limited edition version for China
description: For ten days only, fans of actress Wang Churan can pick up a limited edition version of the Honor 600 Pro for China. The promo will run from July 21 to 31 and is available through authorized partner stores in the country. Honor 600 Pro (China) Flash Limited Edition Gift Box The “Flash Limited Edition Gift Box” includes the phone in Sky Blue color, a quicksand phone case, a 10,000mAh power bank, plus extra goodies like a tote bag, limited edition stickers and a Wang Churan flash card. The Honor 600 Pro 12/256GB is available with a CNY 200 discount on its regular price, so it...

title: Xiaomi reportedly ups its yearly smartphone sales target as it senses memory market normalization
description: The surge in memory/storage prices due to the ongoing supply shortage has led to higher smartphone prices, which has also affected the business. Xiaomi, for example, shipped 33.8 million smartphones in Q1 2026, a 19.2% decline from Q1 2025. However, a new report claims that Xiaomi is expecting the situation to improve as it has raised its yearly smartphone sales target from around 90 million to 110 million units. The report, citing supply chain sources, states that most of the additional shipment volume is expected to come from entry-level devices. A person familiar with the matter...

title: Samsung creates Robotics eXperience (RX) division
description: Samsung Electronics has established a Robotics eXperience (RX) Business Promotion Office that will be led by CEO TM Roh. This division will consolidate Samsung’s existing robotic research and development efforts. Lee Dong-geon, who previously led robot strategies for Boston Dynamics while he was at Hyundai, will serve as the head of the Robotics Strategy Team. The RX division will be headquartered at Samsung’s Seoul R&D Campus in Umyeon-dong, but the company will open research hubs in the US, China and Japan. Samsung’s plan is to focus on industrial robots first – it wants to use...

title: Watch the Samsung Galaxy Unpacked event live here
description: The day is finally here - Samsung is set to host its Galaxy Unpacked event today, in London, UK. The company is expected to unveil its next-generation Galaxy Z foldables, including a new Galaxy Z Fold model with a wide form-factor, alongside the latest Galaxy Watch lineup. The event will be livestreamed on Samsung's official website and YouTube channel, starting at 9:00 AM EDT, 3:00 PM CET, 6:30 PM IST, and 1:00 PM UTC. You can also watch the keynote live using the embedded stream below. Want to know what Samsung has in store for Galaxy Unpacked? Check out our detailed roundup of...
